summary: Closed-unmerged, Claude-generated PR proposing a batch of pointer-math-heavy decompilations across gm, gr, and it code. The PR body says the functions used M2C_FIELD, raw pointer arithmetic, and u8* casts and were grouped separately because match quality versus code style might need discussion. It also bundled large new local automation/docs files. The slice has no reviewer comments and no explicit closure reason, so treat the implementations as experimental/unaccepted evidence rather than merged project practice.

key_files.role: Replaced stubs for fn_80179F84 and fn_8017A9B4 using HSD_JObj child/next traversal, result-player globals, HSD_ImageDesc setup, and byte-offset table lookup.
Split lbl_80472D28 padding to expose s16 x10A and implemented fn_8017F14C with a capped value read from arg0+0x98.
Implemented grCastle_801CD960 and grCastle_801CF750 with Ground byte-offset math, a 12-entry loop, CollData flag check, and PAD_STACK.
Changed grMaterial_801C9470/801C9490 to Item_GObj plus CommandInfo, implemented material command value extraction and ColorOverlay callback setup in grMaterial_801C9604/801C9698.
Implemented fn_802010A4 as a 17-entry table scan using gp->gv.pad_0+0x6C and bit extraction from arg2[0x34].
Implemented it_802CA49C, it_802CA6A0, and itKamex_UnkMotion1_Phys using Pokemon item vars, item callbacks, M2C_FIELD offsets 0xE3C/0xE40, and fall-speed physics.
Added itGShell_HurtInit and implemented five Z/green shell functions covering init, entered-air transition, two collision callbacks, and hurt-capsule setup.
Updated several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations to concrete return and parameter types, including gmresultplayer, gmregclear, grrcruise, and grmaterial headers.
Added unmerged Claude/decomp workflow documentation plus overnight automation, stub finding, stream-log parsing, progress tracking, and scripts/logs/ gitignore entry.

what_changed: The PR body claims 18 decompiled pointer-math functions; the diff visibly replaces 17 stub markers plus changes the existing grMaterial_801C9470 wrapper signature. The named targets are fn_80179F84, fn_8017A9B4, fn_802010A4, fn_8017F14C, grMaterial_801C9490, grMaterial_801C9698, grMaterial_801C9604, grCastle_801CD960, grCastle_801CF750, it_802CA49C, it_802CA6A0, itKamex_UnkMotion1_Phys, and five itzgshell functions.

gmresultplayer gained extern declarations for lbl_8046E1B0, lbl_8046E38C, lbl_8046E39C, lbl_8046E3AC, an HSD_TObj include, a local bitfield struct for lbl_8046E3AC, JObj sibling capture in fn_80179F84, and HSD_ImageDesc table setup in fn_8017A9B4.
gmregclear exposed lbl_80472D28.x10A inside prior padding and used it as a multiplier in fn_8017F14C after checking gm_8016AE38()->x24C8.x4_7 and capping a value from arg0+0x98 at 9999.

Stage changes used raw Ground offsets for castle collision/material state: grCastle_801CD960 iterates 12 records at gp+0xE0+i*0x14; grCastle_801CF750 conditionally increments a float at gp+idx*16+0xD0; grMaterial_801C9490 writes gp+0xC0 and flags b6; grMaterial_801C9604/9698 configure ColorOverlay at gp+0x40.

grrcruise fn_802010A4 scans 17 entries from a pointer stored under gp->gv.pad_0+0x6C, matches a s16 id at entry+2, initializes entry+4/entry[0], and increments entry+8 when a collision flag nibble indicates value 1.
itkamex changes initialize Pokemon item state and callbacks and read/write item/article special attributes through M2C_FIELD at offsets 0x4, 0x18, 0x1C, 0xE3C, and 0xE40.

itzgshell changes add a local itGShell_HurtInit struct, include itcoll.h, reset shell velocities/state on init, handle entered-air transitions, share similar GA_Air collision logic for motion 10/11, and build a HurtCapsule from item offset 0xE2C.

The PR adds large automation/documentation files unrelated to the C stubs: CLAUDE.md, decomp_overnight.sh, decomp_helpers.py, find_stubs.py, find_all_stubs.sh, schedule_overnight.sh, and a scripts/logs/ ignore rule.
smart_moves: The author explicitly isolated these as pointer-math-heavy cases and warned they may need match-quality versus style discussion, which is useful triage for reviewers.
The diff updates headers from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete types where possible instead of only changing C bodies.
Some raw pads were narrowed into named fields or small structs, notably lbl_80472D28.x10A and itGShell_HurtInit, showing a path from offset-based matches toward typed structure recovery.
Known project types were used where available: HSD_JObj, HSD_ImageDesc, Ground, CollData, ColorOverlay, CommandInfo, Item_GObj, Article, Item, and HurtCapsule.
The implementations preserve recognizable high-level loop/table shapes instead of fully unrolled assembly: JObj sibling iteration, a 12-record castle loop, and a 17-entry Rainbow Cruise table scan.

The tooling additions, while unmerged and broad, tried to make automation more testable by moving shell-inline Python into decomp_helpers.py subcommands and parsing Claude stream JSON result events structurally instead of relying only on grep-like text matching.
decomp_lessons: Raw pointer arithmetic can be useful as a matching scaffold, but this PR demonstrates why it should usually be converted into proper structs/fields before merge; examples include M2C_FIELD(ip, ..., 0xE3C/0xE40), (u8*) gp + offsets, and gp->gv.pad_0 + 0x6C.

When a function match depends on an unknown byte offset, split pads surgically and name the discovered member with its offset, as done with lbl_80472D28.x10A; future work should continue by identifying surrounding fields rather than leaving long-term casts.
Changing callback signatures can uncover the actual data flow: grMaterial_801C9470/801C9490 moved from HSD_JObj* to Item_GObj*, CommandInfo*, which made the command bytecode extraction visible.

Melee decomp PRs should keep scope reviewable. This PR mixed C decompilation with over 1,200 lines of Claude/overnight tooling and docs; there is no reviewer statement, but future work should likely split automation/docs from function matches.
Verify PR body counts against the diff. Here the title/body say 18 functions, while the provided list and stub replacements account for 17 plus one existing wrapper signature change.
The added CLAUDE.md itself says to avoid raw pointer arithmetic and prefer true matches/proper structs; that guidance conflicts with much of the C diff and captures the central tension of this PR.
naming_conventions: Stub markers use the project form /// #function_name, and replacement functions retain address-derived names when no semantic name is known, e.g. fn_8017F14C and fn_802010A4.
Headers and local prototypes use address comments such as /* 17F14C */ and /* 1C9470 */ before declarations.
Module prefixes visible in the diff: gm for game mode/results/register clear, gr for ground/stage code, and it for item code; unnamed functions keep fn_ plus address.

The added CLAUDE.md states project naming guidance: file names lowerCamelCase, function names UpperCamelCase with file/module prefixes, and standard-library reimplementations lowercase. Because the PR was unmerged, treat this as author-added guidance, not evidence of acceptance from this PR.
Struct and pad fields follow offset-bearing names such as x10A, pad_10C, x0_4, xE1C_b0, and comments like /* 0x00 */ in itGShell_HurtInit.
Item motion/callback names follow existing style such as itKamex_UnkMotion1_Phys, itZrshell_UnkMotion10_Coll, itZrshell_UnkMotion11_Coll, and it_2725_Logic11_EnteredAir.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(16) in grCastle_801CF750 and it_802CA49C, and PAD_STACK(8) in it_802DE0F0, indicating stack-size matching pressure.
Used M2C_FIELD for unresolved item/article fields: ip+0xE3C, ip+0xE40, ip+0xE2C, attrs+0x4, attrs+0x18, and attrs+0x1C.
Used raw u8* casts for addressable struct offsets: arg0+0x98, gp+0xE0+i*0x14, gp+idx*16+0xD0, gp+0xC0, gp+0x40, and table entries at entry+0/2/4/8.
Matched branch/bitfield idioms with explicit casts and bit extraction: ((arg2[0x34] >> 3) & 0xF) != 1, cd->x34_flags.b1234 == 1, and (*(u16*) cmd->ptr[0] >> 2) & 0xFF.
For fn_80179F84, used a pointer-to-pointer cursor over lbl_8046E39C and *++p assignment to store up to four JObj child/next pointers while handling NULL child chains.
For fn_8017A9B4, built HSD_ImageDesc pointers at base+0x104/base+0x164 plus slot*0x18, cleared image_ptr, then called lb_800121FC with u16 table values from lookup-derived tbl offsets.
For grMaterial_801C9604/9698, mapped Ground+0x40 to ColorOverlay and used lb_80014258 with fn_801C9664 as a callback to set gp->x10_flags.b4 on completion.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews are present in the slice; there is no explicit human reviewer feedback to extract.
The PR was closed unmerged about four minutes after creation, but the dump gives no closure rationale.
The only style warning in the evidence is author-supplied in the PR body: these pointer-math patterns were grouped separately because they may need discussion on match quality versus code style.
follow_up_queries: Search later merged PRs for fn_80179F84, fn_8017A9B4, fn_8017F14C, and fn_802010A4 to see whether these pointer-math implementations were rewritten into typed structs.
Look for accepted definitions of item fields around Item offsets 0xE2C, 0xE3C, and 0xE40 in itkamex/itzgshell before reusing M2C_FIELD forms.
Investigate the Rainbow Cruise table behind gp->gv.pad_0+0x6C and whether it has a named struct replacing entry+0/2/4/8 accesses.
Check grMaterial_801C9470/801C9490 history for the accepted callback signature involving Item_GObj and CommandInfo.
Search for a later accepted itGShell_HurtInit or HurtCapsule initialization pattern before reusing the local struct from this unmerged PR.
If closure reason matters, inspect GitHub events or author follow-up PRs; this dump contains no review explanation.
